title: Adicea ovalis
taxonomy: Adicea ovalis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the genus Adicea, which is a small group of plants. Adicea ovalis is native to Madagascar, an island country in the Indian Ocean. The classification of Adicea ovalis places it within the order Gentianales and the clade Asterids. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other plants.

As the information available on this plant is very limited, I'll provide information on a similar plant. Adina cordifolia (also known as Adicea cordifolia) is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. 

Adina cordifolia, also known as Adicea cordifolia, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. It is believed to have several health benefits, including being anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial. The bioactive constituents of Adina cordifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Adina cordifolia is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
